summary.intent: Implement GitHub issues #268 and #274 by adding Meter, Stat, and Progress agnostic core APIs to the ars-components data-display module, including tests, snapshots, spec-conformance, and mutation coverage, then ship a non-draft PR.
summary.outcome: All three components were implemented TDD-style with passing focused tests, spec-conformance, proptests, and clean mutation coverage (Meter 51 caught/0 missed, Stat 30/0, Progress 79/0). A non-draft PR with Closes #268/#274 and co-author trailer was created after cargo xci-fast passed all 10 gates.

repo: ComponentPart derive macro already derives required traits; adding explicit derives (Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash) conflicts and causes compile errors
cargo mutants baseline fails without --features i18n due to a pre-existing utility/highlight proptest that references i18n-gated imports outside a cfg guard
Post-implementation audit requires two consecutive clean rounds before publishing; each round's findings must be fixed and re-verified before counting clean
Meter spec uses data-ars-segment while issue #268 named data-ars-zone; both must be emitted to satisfy both contracts simultaneously
Progress uses a directory-backed module (progress/mod.rs) because state machines grow test surface; flat files are used for stateless connect components like Meter and Stat
cargo xci-fast runs 10 sequential gates (fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapters, ICU4X, web-intl) and must pass before commit/push
Message closures use an Arc pattern, not plain Fn trait objects
Adapter spec sketches (Leptos/Dioxus) must be kept in sync with core API signatures; stale Api::new() call shapes were found and fixed during the audit

code.finding: Part::CircleRange { radius: f64 } cannot auto-derive Eq + Hash due to f64; requires a manual ComponentPart impl
format_options was declared in Props but the default value_text path ignored it; audit caught this drift between Props and connect output
Progress proptest must constrain SetMax generator so max > min; arbitrary values violate the component contract and produce spurious failures
Controlled Progress value sync: SetValue events must route through the requested-value field, not stale Bindable::set storage, to keep is_indeterminate consistent with the controlled prop

workflow: cargo test does not accept multiple test-filter arguments in one invocation; the plan's multi-filter commands had to be rewritten as a single data_display:: prefix filter plus a separate integration test binary call
cargo mutants must be run per-file with -f, not across directories, and requires --features i18n for this project to pass its baseline compile
cargo insta accept followed by --unreferenced=reject is the correct snapshot workflow: accept new snapshots first, then gate against orphans in a second pass
Mutation triage loop (run → read missed.txt → add targeted assertions → rerun) is more efficient than broad snapshot churn for closing coverage gaps
GitHub Project V2 item status updates require four IDs (project PVT_..., item PVTI_..., field, option); resolve them with gh project item-list and gh project field-list before calling item-edit

friction: cargo mutants without --features i18n fails the entire baseline due to a pre-existing utility/highlight proptest outside a cfg guard; required deviating from the plan's mutation commands
Plan's cargo test commands with multiple test filters are invalid Cargo syntax and had to be rewritten as equivalent single-prefix commands

ComponentPart derive macro conflict: clippy suggested adding Copy/Clone derives that the macro already emits, causing a compile error that required reverting and switching to borrowed part refs instead
cargo xci-fast took substantial wall-clock time across 10 gates before the commit could proceed
Meter issue/spec attribute name mismatch (data-ars-zone vs data-ars-segment) required emitting both attrs rather than a single source of truth
f64 in Part::CircleRange forced a manual ComponentPart impl instead of a clean derive, adding boilerplate
open_items: Adapter components (Leptos, Dioxus) for Meter, Stat, and Progress are explicitly out of scope and not yet implemented
Stat currency prop sketch exists in the spec but was not implemented (not in canonical Props block; issue said no spec change required)
Pre-existing utility/highlight proptest default-feature compile issue was not fixed as it is outside the task scope
Global snapshot-count lint warning pre-dates this PR and was not resolved
CI green status and Codex thumbs-up review loop were not yet complete at session end
